LanguageData.VFES_PlayerSettlement.description: A small hideout of gang members and criminals.
LanguageData.VFES_PlayerSettlement.label: new hideout
LanguageData.SettlerCivil.description:
These people have lived here for decades or centuries, and have lost most of the technology that brought them to this world. They are more than capable of defending themselves with basic gunpowder weaponry whilst they go about their every day routine of herding cattle, drinking chemshine and hunting down bounties.

They are concerned with the practical matters of trade, trust, and survival.

This particular group holds civil behavior in high regard.
LanguageData.SettlerCivil.label: civil settlers
LanguageData.SettlerRough.description:
These people have lived here for decades or centuries, and have lost most of the technology that brought them to this world. They are more than capable of defending themselves with basic gunpowder weaponry whilst they go about their every day routine of herding cattle, drinking chemshine and hunting down bounties.

They are not concerned with the practical matters of trade and trust.

This particular group believes that noone is created equal, and only strength allows one to survive on the frontier.
LanguageData.SettlerRough.label: rough settlers
LanguageData.SettlerSavage.description: These people have lived here for decades or centuries, and have lost most of the technology that brought them to this world. They are a loose confederation of bandit gangs who've agreed to mostly fight outsiders instead of fighting each other.\n\nBandits don't sow, they don't build, and they rarely trade. Driven by a blood-and-honor culture that values personal strength and ruthlessness, they enrich themselves by raiding and robbing their more productive neighbors.
LanguageData.SettlerSavage.label: bandits
LanguageData.GR_RoamingMonstrosities.description: The effect of hundreds of years of genetic experimentation running amok. Hordes of ravenous, maddened genetic aberrations are a threat to anyone in this planet.
LanguageData.GR_RoamingMonstrosities.label: roaming monstrosities
LanguageData.VFEE_Deserters.description: An organised group of imperial deserters who decided to actively disrupt the organisation of the Empire.
LanguageData.VFEE_Deserters.label: deserters
LanguageData.VFEE_NewFamily.description: A colony of a royal family.
LanguageData.VFEE_NewFamily.label: new family
LanguageData.VFED_NewSafehaven.label: new safehaven
LanguageData.VFET_WildMen.description: A player-controlled gathering of wild men.
LanguageData.VFET_WildMen.label: wild men
LanguageData.AM_EnemyPirate.label: pirate scavenger
LanguageData.AM_PlayerColony.description: Poor scavengers who found an underground safe house.
LanguageData.AM_PlayerColony.label: safe house
LanguageData.VFEM2_CivilClan.description:
These clanmen have been here for a long time. Perhaps their ancestors crashed here a thousand years ago, as their legends suggest. In any case, the nordmen are fierce people who live off of the work of others, and they are not afraid to use force to take what's not theirs.

Albeit not as technologically advanced as other factions, the nordmen can be a valuable ally and a terrible enemy due to their hardiness and savage, heathen ways.

This particular clan is more progressive than others, and is more likely to engage in trade and diplomacy with other factions.
LanguageData.VFEM2_CivilClan.label: civil clan
LanguageData.VFEM2_ClanRough.description:
These clanmen have been here for a long time. Perhaps their ancestors crashed here a thousand years ago, as their legends suggest. In any case, the nordmen are fierce people who live off of the work of others, and they are not afraid to use force to take what's not theirs.

Albeit not as technologically advanced as other factions, the nordmen can be a valuable ally and a terrible enemy due to their hardiness and savage, heathen ways.

This particular clan values warlike dominance; it may be difficult to turn them into an ally.
LanguageData.VFEM2_ClanRough.label: rough clan
LanguageData.VFEM2_ClanSavage.description:
These clanmen have been here for a long time. Perhaps their ancestors crashed here a thousand years ago, as their legends suggest. In any case, the nordmen are fierce people who live off of the work of others, and they are not afraid to use force to take what's not theirs.

Albeit not as technologically advanced as other factions, the nordmen can be a valuable ally and a terrible enemy due to their hardiness and savage, heathen ways.

This particular clan wreaks havoc in surrounding settlements and takes any survivors into slavery - it will be impossible to become their ally!
LanguageData.VFEM2_ClanSavage.label: savage clan
LanguageData.VFEM2_KingdomCivil.description:
These people have been here for a shorter period of time than their neolithic bretheren. It is believed that some tribespeople and outlanders had come to embrace the medieval ways of life and decided to unite.

Though still technologically inferior to many other factions, the subjects of this kingdom are well-known for using towering fortifications to protect their assets, as well as adopting the use of shields in combat and fighting in a particularly orderly manner.

This particular kingdom is open to keeping trade relations good with other settlements to bolster their economic position.
LanguageData.VFEM2_KingdomCivil.label: civil kingdom
LanguageData.VFEM2_KingdomRough.description:
These people have been here for a shorter period of time than their neolithic bretheren. It is believed that some tribespeople and outlanders had come to embrace the medieval ways of life and decided to unite.

Though still technologically inferior to many other factions, the subjects of this kingdom are well-known for using towering fortifications to protect their assets, as well as adopting the use of shields in combat and fighting in a particularly orderly manner.

This particular kingdom is ruled under a heavy hand and wishes to expand by laying the less fortunate to waste - though it is still possible to befriend them.
LanguageData.VFEM2_KingdomRough.label: rough kingdom
LanguageData.VFEM2_KingdomSavage.description:
These people have been here for a shorter period of time than their neolithic bretheren. It is believed that some tribespeople and outlanders had come to embrace the medieval ways of life and decided to unite.

Though still technologically inferior to many other factions, the subjects of this kingdom are well-known for using towering fortifications to protect their assets, as well as adopting the use of shields in combat and fighting in a particularly orderly manner.

This particular kingdom is driven by a warband styled culture; you will not be able to ally with them!
LanguageData.VFEM2_KingdomSavage.label: savage kingdom
LanguageData.VFEM2_MerchantGuild.description:
These people have been here for a shorter period of time than their neolithic brethren. These wandering traders and skilled negotiators form a vast network of traveling caravans and temporary markets. Originating from a loose coalition of outlanders and former city-dwellers, they abandoned static settlements in favor of constant movement, seeking prosperity through commerce rather than conquest.

Unlike most factions, the Guild operates on a pure barter economy—silver holds no value to them, only goods and services do. Their traders travel far and wide, setting up bustling market hubs wherever they stop, offering rare wares and exotic goods in exchange for what they deem useful.

Though they avoid direct conflict and prefer diplomacy, their well-armed guards ensure that their caravans remain safe from opportunistic raiders.
LanguageData.VFEM2_MerchantGuild.label: merchant's guild
LanguageData.VFEM2_PlayerKingdom.label: new kingdom
LanguageData.VQE_NewVaultPlayerFaction.description: A secret ancient vault hiding a mysterious past.
LanguageData.VQE_NewVaultPlayerFaction.label: new vault
